questions.question: State Theorem 10.1.
questions.answer: The tangent at any point of a circle is perpendicular to the radius through the point of contact.

questions.question: State Theorem 10.2.
questions.answer: The lengths of tangents drawn from an external point to a circle are equal.

questions.question: Which congruence rule proves equality of tangents?
questions.answer: RHS congruence rule.

questions.question: Why are angles between radius and tangent right angles?
questions.answer: Because radius is perpendicular to tangent.

questions.question: Why is OP common in tangent proof?
questions.answer: It is a common side in both triangles.

questions.question: Why are triangles formed in tangent proof congruent?
questions.answer: They satisfy RHS conditions.

questions.question: Why is OQ equal to OR in tangent theorem?
questions.answer: Both are radii of the same circle.

questions.question: Why does OP bisect angle between tangents?
questions.answer: Because corresponding triangles are congruent.

questions.question: What geometric principle ensures shortest distance?
questions.answer: Perpendicular distance theorem.

questions.question: Why is a tangent never a secant?
questions.answer: Because it touches at only one point.

questions.question: Why are two tangents symmetrical?
questions.answer: Because they are equal in length.

questions.question: Why does centre lie on angle bisector of tangents?
questions.answer: Because OP bisects the angle between tangents.

questions.question: Why is perpendicularity crucial in tangent proofs?
questions.answer: It establishes right triangles.

questions.question: Why is tangent length measurable?
questions.answer: Because it is a line segment from external point.

questions.question: Why does tangent not pass through centre?
questions.answer: Because centre lies inside the circle.

questions.question: What condition makes two tangents equal?
questions.answer: They are drawn from the same external point.

questions.question: Why is tangent touching at two points impossible?
questions.answer: It would become a secant.

questions.question: Why is tangent contact always unique?
questions.answer: Only one perpendicular can be drawn at a point.

questions.question: Why does tangent not intersect interior?
questions.answer: Because it only touches the boundary.

questions.question: Why is the radius shortest distance to tangent?
questions.answer: Because it is perpendicular.

questions.question: Why is tangent geometry rigid?
questions.answer: Because it depends on fixed perpendicular relations.

questions.question: Why is tangent theorem fundamental?
questions.answer: It defines tangent-radius relationship.

questions.question: Why is centre equidistant from contact points?
questions.answer: Because both distances are radii.

questions.question: Why are tangent properties used in constructions?
questions.answer: Because of fixed perpendicular nature.

questions.question: Why is tangent concept essential in circles?
questions.answer: It explains boundary contact behavior.
